description_long: This object is a compact arcade-style joystick controller, featuring a rectangular base with a dark blue top surface bordered by a gray frame. On the left side, a prominent red spherical joystick is mounted on a slender metallic shaft, set into a black circular base. To the right of the joystick, there are six large, round, yellow buttons arranged in two rows of three, each slightly raised above the surface for tactile feedback. The base appears solid and sturdy, likely made of plastic or metal, designed to rest securely on a flat surface. The overall design is minimalistic, with no visible branding or display screens, focusing on manual input through the joystick and buttons. This type of controller is commonly used for arcade games or as a specialized input device for gaming in urban entertainment venues or home setups.
description: A compact arcade joystick controller with a red ball-top joystick and six yellow buttons on a blue and gray rectangular base. Designed for manual gaming input, it has a sturdy, minimalistic build.
description_view_0: Front view showing the joystick on the left and all six yellow buttons clearly arranged on the blue surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the height of the joystick and the staggered arrangement of the buttons.
description_view_2: Rear view with the joystick on the right and the buttons visible from behind, showing the rectangular shape of the base.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the joystick's prominence and the spatial layout of the buttons on the surface.
